higher_order_object.name: inventory_conditioned_stratified_path_bundle
higher_order_object.high_level_form: H(I) = (B_family, E_concept, F_attr, F_rel, F_stress, P_path, O_overlap)
higher_order_object.meaning: inventory should be viewed as a higher-order bundle object: base family charts plus multiple attached fibers and admissible path structure

bundle_layers.E_concept: concept-entry section over each family patch
bundle_layers.P_path: admissible path bundle controlling transitions to memory/readout/relation
bundle_layers.O_overlap: restricted overlap bundle controlling valid chart-to-chart transport

candidate_equations.path_bundle: P_path(c, mode_1 -> mode_2) = {gamma : gamma stays inside A(I) and M_feas(I)}

why_higher_order.core_answer: The inventory is no longer well-described by a flat atlas alone. It behaves like a stratified base manifold with multiple attached fibers and path constraints.
evidence: each family patch is low-rank and has its own reusable local basis
different families still reuse a smaller common scaffold of dimensions
object-memory overlap is wide, object-disc overlap is narrow, object-relation overlap is medium

Brain encoding may be governed by an admissible-update intersection A and a stratified viability manifold M_feas; coding forms when local plasticity stays inside A and system trajectories remain inside M_feas.

mathematical_consequences.not_just_points: concepts are not only points z_c, but sections with attached fibers
mathematical_consequences.not_just_vectors: updates are not only vectors, but admissible paths through bundle charts
mathematical_consequences.not_just_one_space: readout, relation, and memory do not live in one flat shared space; they are coupled through overlap bundles

verdict.core_answer: The next theoretical upgrade is to treat the encoding inventory as a stratified path bundle rather than only a layered atlas.
verdict.next_theory_target: derive a compact unified theory from this bundle view and use it to propose falsifiable operator-form changes
